title: Inconsistent behavior of ALTER MODIFY COLUMN DEFAULT on missing columns after OPTIMIZE FINAL
description:
### Company or project name
.
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
There is an inconsistency when ClickHouse handles default values for columns that are added and then merged via OPTIMIZE FINAL. The previously merged column values are not affected by subsequent MODIFY COLUMN DEFAULT operations. The default only applies to new inserts.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
all
### How to reproduce
It's reproduceable in current master.
```sql
CREATE TABLE x (i Int32) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY ();
INSERT INTO x VALUES (1);
ALTER TABLE x ADD COLUMN k Int32 DEFAULT 5;
SELECT k FROM x;  -- returns: 1, 5
ALTER TABLE x MODIFY COLUMN k DEFAULT 10;
SELECT k FROM x;  -- returns: 1, 10
OPTIMIZE TABLE x FINAL;
ALTER TABLE x MODIFY COLUMN k DEFAULT 15;
SELECT k FROM x;  -- returns: 1, 10, expected: 1, 15
```
### Expected behavior
After modifying the column default to 15, SELECT k FROM x should reflect the new default for rows that were previously merged as missing column values.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
Once the missing column k is merged during the OPTIMIZE FINAL, it stores the old default value (10 in this example), which prevents future default modifications from applying to existing rows. This is inconsistent with the behavior before the merge.
I'm planning to resolve this in #88860, which will also partially address #4968.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE y (a INT, b VARCHAR(255));
ALTER TABLE y ADD COLUMN c DOUBLE PRECISION DEFAULT 3.14;
UPDATE y SET c = 2.71 WHERE c IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: ALTER MODIFY COLUMN DEFAULT after OPTIMIZE FINAL inconsistency
